title: これKernelに導入できる？iphoneで開発引き継げる？Kernelで精査して。
summary_text: **Findings** 1. **High: refresh mutex is instance-local only, so multi-tab race remains** - `refreshInFlight` is scoped inside one `createEndpointClient()` instance, not shared across tabs/clients. [happy-endpoint-client.js](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/apps/happy-web/src/adapters/happy-endpoint-client.js#L36) [happy-endpoint-client.js](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/apps/happy-web/src/adapters/happy-endpoint-client.js#L79) - On simultaneous `401`, different tabs can both call refresh with the same old refresh token; backend consume-before-rotate makes one fail. [happy-endpoint-client.js](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/apps/happy-web/src/adapters/happy-endpoint-client.js#L92) 2. **Medium: refreshed tokens are not shared cross-tab** - New tokens are written only to in-memory `config`, so other tabs keep stale `authToken/refreshToken` and can trigger failed refresh. [happy-endpoint-client.js](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/apps/happy-web/src/adapters/happy-endpoint-client.js#L74) 3. **Test gap: current test proves only same-instance concurrency** - Existing concurrency test uses one client instance (`Promise.all` on same object), so it misses multi-client/multi-tab behavior. [test-endpoint-client.js](/Users/masayuki/Dev/fugue-orchestrator/apps/happy-web/tests/test-endpoint-client.js#L66) **Smallest concrete implementation change (no edit performed)** - Keep current local `refreshInFlight`, add **cross-tab guard + token sync** in this file only: 1. Use `navigator.locks.request("happy-auth-refresh", ...)` when available. 2. Store refreshed tokens in `localStorage` (single key, e.g. `happy-auth-tokens` with timestamp). 3. Before refreshing, read shared tokens; if token already changed, skip refresh and reuse shared token. 4. After successful refresh, persist shared tokens; keep current fallback path when `navigator.locks` is unavailable. This is minimal surface area: one adapter file, no API changes. **Smallest concrete test change** - Add one test in `test-endpoint-client.js` with **two client instances** and separate config objects: 1. Mock `navigator.locks.request` as global cross-client mutex. 2. Mock shared `localStorage`. 3. Simulate both clients hitting `401` concurrently. 4. Assert both recover successfully and `refreshCalls === 1`. Residual risk after this minimal fix: browsers without Web Locks will still rely on per-instance mutex (current behavior), but modern browsers/tabs get race closure.
